per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I get a little feverish sometimes, usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius, but it's not super high and it comes and goes. And I've also been getting night sweats, which is really weird because I don't normally sweat that much at night.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't usually feel like I'm going to pass out or anything, and I haven't noticed any excessive sweating except for at night. But to be honest, it's kind of hard to get comfortable at all when my chest feels so tight and wheezy... that's a big part of why I've been having trouble sleeping.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I haven't really had any nausea or stomach issues, but I've definitely lost my appetite over the past few days. Even thinking about food makes me feel a bit queasy, which is unusual for me because I usually love cooking and trying new recipes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've already told you about my feverish episodes and the night sweats. The fever is usually around 38.2 degrees Celsius and comes and goes. And yeah, the night sweats have been pretty consistent for a few nights now. It's just really weird because I don't normally sweat that much at night.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I try to eat healthy most of the time, but lately, my appetite has been so low that even thinking about food is exhausting. Usually, I'd have oatmeal with fruit for breakfast, a sandwich or leftovers for lunch at work, and maybe some stir-fry or roasted vegetables for dinner with my partner. But now, I'm just not hungry at all. It's really weird because cooking and trying new recipes are things I usually enjoy.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: You asked me about my mom earlier, and I didn't mention her asthma. Yeah, she's been managing it with medication for years, but that's all I know. And actually, my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer when I was a kid, so there might be some concern there too...
